findings.description: CVE-2017-7659: A maliciously constructed HTTP/2 request could cause mod_http2 in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.24, 2.4.25 to dereference a NULL pointer and crash the server process.
findings.solution: Update Apache to the latest stable version. Reference: http://www.debian.org/security/2017/dsa-3896
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Apache from version 2.4.25 to the latest stable release to patch CVE-2017-7659.
findings.business_impact: This known vulnerability (CVE-2017-7659) in Apache 2.4.25 may allow attackers to compromise the system. CVSS score 7.5 indicates high risk.

findings.description: CVE-2016-8743: Apache HTTP Server, in all releases prior to 2.2.32 and 2.4.25, was liberal in the whitespace accepted from requests and sent in response lines and headers. Accepting these different behaviors represented a security concern when httpd participates in any chain of proxies or interacts with back-end application servers, either through mod_proxy or using conventional CGI mechanisms, and may result in request smuggling, response splitting and cache pollution.
findings.solution: Update Apache to the latest stable version. Reference: http://rhn.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2017-1415.html
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Apache from version 2.4.25 to the latest stable release to patch CVE-2016-8743.
findings.business_impact: This known vulnerability (CVE-2016-8743) in Apache 2.4.25 may allow attackers to compromise the system. CVSS score 7.5 indicates high risk.

findings.description: CVE-2015-6527: The php_str_replace_in_subject function in ext/standard/string.c in PHP 7.x before 7.0.0 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted value in the third argument to the str_ireplace function.
findings.solution: Update PHP to the latest stable version. Reference: http://git.php.net/?p=php-src.git%3Ba=commit%3Bh=6aeee47b2cd47915ccfa3b41433a3f57aea24dd5
findings.recommendation: Upgrade PHP from version 7.0.0 to the latest stable release to patch CVE-2015-6527.
findings.business_impact: This known vulnerability (CVE-2015-6527) in PHP 7.0.0 may allow attackers to compromise the system. CVSS score 7.3 indicates high risk.

findings.description: CVE-2012-2053: The sudoers file in the Linux system configuration in F5 FirePass 6.0.0 through 6.1.0 and 7.0.0 does not require a password for executing commands as root, which allows local users to gain privileges via the sudo program, as demonstrated by the user account that executes PHP scripts, a different vulnerability than CVE-2012-1777.
findings.solution: Update PHP to the latest stable version. Reference: https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/74813
findings.recommendation: Upgrade PHP from version 7.0.0 to the latest stable release to patch CVE-2012-2053.
findings.business_impact: This known vulnerability (CVE-2012-2053) in PHP 7.0.0 may allow attackers to compromise the system. CVSS score 7.2 indicates high risk.

findings.description: CVE-2025-68129: Auth0-PHP is a PHP SDK for Auth0 Authentication and Management APIs. In applications built with the Auth0-PHP SDK, the audience validation in access tokens is performed improperly. Without proper validation, affected applications may accept ID tokens as Access tokens. Projects are affected if they use Auth0-PHP SDK versions between v8.0.0 and v8.17.0, or applications using the following SDKs that rely on the Auth0-PHP SDK versions between v8.0.0 and v8.17.0: Auth0/symfony versions between 5.0.0
findings.solution: Update PHP to the latest stable version. Reference: https://github.com/auth0/auth0-PHP/commit/7fe700053aee609718460c123f00f53c511f0f7f
findings.recommendation: Upgrade PHP from version 7.0.0 to the latest stable release to patch CVE-2025-68129.
findings.business_impact: This known vulnerability (CVE-2025-68129) in PHP 7.0.0 may allow attackers to compromise the system. CVSS score 6.8 indicates medium risk.

findings.description: CVE-2016-4975: Possible CRLF injection allowing HTTP response splitting attacks for sites which use mod_userdir. This issue was mitigated by changes made in 2.4.25 and 2.2.32 which prohibit CR or LF injection into the "Location" or other outbound header key or value. Fixed in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.25 (Affected 2.4.1-2.4.23). Fixed in Apache HTTP Server 2.2.32 (Affected 2.2.0-2.2.31).
findings.solution: Update Apache to the latest stable version. Reference: http://www.securityfocus.com/bid/105093
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Apache from version 2.4.25 to the latest stable release to patch CVE-2016-4975.
findings.business_impact: This known vulnerability (CVE-2016-4975) in Apache 2.4.25 may allow attackers to compromise the system. CVSS score 6.1 indicates medium risk.
